channel.title: Cookies on Bell Favorite Recipes
channel.description: Recent content in Cookies on Bell Favorite Recipes

item.title: Traditional Gingerbread Man Cookies
item.description:
Submitted by Stephanie and Shawn Thomas

Cousins of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 5 to 5 ½ cups all-purpose flour
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 2 tsp ginger
  • 2 tsp cinnamon
  • 1 tsp nutmeg
  • 1 tsp cloves
  • 1 cup shortening
  • 1 cup sugar
  • 1 ¼ cups unsulphured molasses
  • 2 eggs, beaten

Instructions

Preheat oven to 375 degrees. Thoroughly mix flour, soda, salt and spices. Melt shortening in large saucepan. Cool slightly. Add sugar, molasses and eggs. Mix well. Add four cups of dry ingredients and mix well. Turn mixture onto lightly floured surface. Knead in remaining dry ingredients by hand. Add a little more flour, if necessary, to make a firm dough. Roll out on a lightly floured surface to ¼ inch thickness for cut-out cookies. Bake on an ungreased cookie sheet (small and medium-sized cookies for 6-10 minutes, large cookies for 10-15 minutes). One recipe of this dough will yield 2 to 3 dozen average-sized cookies.

item.title: Refrigerator Balls
item.description:
Submitted by Buddy Pierce

Nephew/Godson of the Groom

Ingredients

  • 1 can of Eaglebrand
  • 3 cups miniature marshmallows
  • ½ cup maraschino cherries, chopped
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • 2 ½ cups graham cracker crumbs

Instructions

Mix all ingredients together. Butter hands and roll into 1 inch balls. Roll in fine coconut.

Refrigerate. Makes about 60.

Uncle Billy’s favorite!!

item.title: Oile Bolen (Dutch Donut)
item.description:
Submitted by Audrey and John Gosson

Cousins of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 3 eggs
  • ½ cup sugar
  • ½ cup corn syrup
  • 4 ½ cups flour
  • 5 tsp baking powder
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 1 tsp nutmeg
  • 3 tbsp melted shortening or salad oil
  • ½ cup milk
  • apples chopped in small pieces, raisins and candied fruit-
  • should equal 2 cups total

Instructions

Beat egg yolks until thick. Add sugar gradually and then corn syrup. Mix shortening or oil with milk and stir into the egg and sugar mixture.

item.title: Granny Gosson's Ginger Sparklers
item.description:
Submitted by Heather Bell

Bride

Ingredients

  • 2 cups flour
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • 2 tsp ginger
  • ¼ cup molasses
  • ¾ cup margarine
  • 1 cup brown sugar
  • 1 egg
  • 1 ½ tsp baking soda
  • ½ tsp cinnamon

Instructions

In a large bowl, cream sugar, margarine, egg and molasses. In a separate bowl, combine dry ingredients. Blend dry ingredients into cream mixture.

Roll batter into balls and place on a greased cookie sheet. Using a fork, gently press down on balls to flatten slightly. Sprinkle cookies with sugar. Bake at 375 degrees for 8-10 minutes.

item.title: Cookie Candies
item.description:
Submitted by Michelle and Jesse Webster

Cousins of the Groom

Ingredients

  • 1 cup soft butter
  • ¾ cup icing sugar
  • 1 tbsp vanilla
  • 2 cups flour & ¾ tsp salt sifted together
  • ½ cup rolled oats
  • ½ cup semi sweet chocolate pieces
  • ½ cup milk
  • sprinkles of your choice

Instructions

Mix butter, sugar and vanilla. Cream well. Add flour mixture (sifted) and rolled oats. With hands, make into balls, roll into logs or use cookie cutters. Cook at 325 degrees for 20 minutes.

item.title: Boiled Raisin Cookies
item.description:
Submitted by Jenny Navid

Aunt of the Bride

  • 2 cups raisins
  • 1 cup water

Boil together for 5 minutes.

Ingredients

  • 4 cups flour
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • 1 tsp baking powder
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 2 cups white sugar
  • 1 cup shortening
  • 3 eggs
  • 1 tsp nutmeg
  • 1 tsp allspice
  • ½ cup chopped nuts
  • 2 tsp cinnamon

Instructions

Mix dry ingredients. Add beaten eggs with the raisins and water. Drop by the tablespoon onto a cookie sheet.

item.title: OREO Cookie Dessert
item.description:
Submitted by Fern Henri

Uncle of the Bride

Ingredients

  • 1 square box of vanilla ice cream
  • 1 container of Cool Whip

Instructions

Cut ice cream up into ½ to 1 inch thick pieces to fill the bottom of a 9 inch square pan or a 9x13 inch pan.

Crush OREO cookies until crumbly. Spread on top of ice cream. Pour chocolate syrup over cookies.

Spread Cool Whip over top. Sprinkle more crushed cookies over Cool Whip to decorate.

item.title: Applesauce Currant Cookies
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup vegetable oil
  • 2 tsp baking powder
  • ¼ cup granulated sugar substitute
  • ½ tsp baking soda
  • 1 egg
  • ½ cup quick-cooking oats, uncooked
  • ½ cup unsweetened applesauce
  • ½ cup currants
  • 1 tsp vanilla extract
  • 1 tsp ground allspice
  • ½ cup whole wheat flour
  • Vegetable cooking spray
  • ½ cup all-purpose flour

Instructions

Combine oil and sugar substitute in a bowl. Add egg; beat at medium speed of an electric mixer until fluffy. Stir in applesauce and vanilla. Combine whole wheat flour and next 6 ingredients; add to oil mixture, stirring well. Cover; chill 15 minutes. Drop by heaping teaspoon onto baking sheets coated with cooking spray. Bake at 375 degrees for 10 minutes.

item.title: Yummy Oatmeal Cookies
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup butter or margarine
  • 1-1/2 cups brown sugar
  • 2 eggs
  • ½ cup milk
  • ½ tsp white vinegar (I (Mom) didn’t use this)
  • 1-3/4 cups all-purpose flour
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • 1 tsp baking powder
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • 1 tsp cinnamon
  • 3 cups quick-cooking rolled oats
  • ½ cup raisins (optional)
  • ½ cup chopped walnuts or sliced almonds (optional)

Instructions

Cream together butter and brown sugar. Add the vinegar to the milk and set it aside for one minute. Add eggs to the butter and sugar mixture one at a time, mixing until light and fluffy. Add the milk with vinegar and mix until well incorporated.

item.title: Whipped Shortbread
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ lb butter
  • ½ lb margarine
  • ½ cup cornstarch
  • 1 cup sifted icing sugar
  • 3 cups sifted flour

Instructions

Cream butter and margarine gradually with cornstarch and sugar. Add flour, a little bit at a time, and whip mixture until it looks like cream. The more they are beat – the better they are.

Drop by spoonfuls on cookie sheet and bake in a 325 degree oven for 22 minutes.

item.title: Thimble Cookies
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup butter
  • 1 cup sifted flour
  • ¼ cup brown sugar
  • Nuts, chopped finely or corn flakes crushed
  • 1 egg, separated
  • Jelly
  • 1 tsp flavouring

Instructions

Cream butter. Add sugar gradually and mix well. Add well Beaten egg yolk, flavouring and flour. Shape into balls about the size of a walnut. Dip in unbeaten egg white, then in finely chopped nuts, almonds, walnuts or soya bean flakes or corn flakes. Dent in center with a thimble and bake in a moderate oven at 350 degrees for 5 minutes and dent again. Bake for 12 to 15 minutes more. Fill with jelly or jam while still hot if planning not to freeze them.

item.title: The Ultimate Chocolate Chip Cookie
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3/4 cup Golden Crisco
  • 1-1/4 cup lightly packed brown sugar
  • 1 egg
  • 2 Tbsp milk
  • 2 tsp vanilla
  • 1-1/2 cups all-purpose flour
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 3/4 tsp baking soda
  • 1 cup chocolate chips
  • 1 cup coarsely chopped pecans or walnuts

Instructions

Preheat oven to 375 degrees.

Cream Golden Crisco and brown sugar in large bowl until light. Add egg, milk and vanilla, beating 1 minute or until well blended.

item.title: Star Sugar Cookies
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup shortening
  • 1 cup sugar
  • 2 eggs
  • 2 tsp vanilla
  • 2-1/2 cups all-purpose flour
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • 2 tsp cream of tartar
  • 1 tsp salt
  • Raisins
  • Fine sugar

Instructions

Heat oven to 375 degrees. Blend shortening and sugar. Beat in eggs and vanilla. Blend in sifted dry ingredients. On lightly floured board, pat or roll dough to ¼-inch thickness. Cut in 3-inch rounds. Arrange on greased cookie sheet. Press 3 raisins in center of each cookie.

item.title: Rolled Cut-Out Sugar Cookies
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3 cups flour
  • 1 cup butter or shortening
  • 1 cup white sugar
  • 2 eggs
  • 1-1/2 tsp baking powder
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • 1 tsp vanilla or lemon extract
  • 1 tbsp milk

Instructions

Cream butter, sugar, eggs and milk. Add vanilla. Stir in sifted dry ingredients and blend well.

Roll out dough ¼” thick and cut in desired shapes. Bake at 375 degrees until done, about 8 – 10 minutes.

item.title: Peanut Butter Cookies
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup shortening
  • 1 cup white sugar
  • 1 cup brown sugar
  • 2 eggs
  • 1 tbsp vanilla
  • 1 cup peanut butter
  • 3 cups flour
  • 2 tsp baking soda
  • 1 tsp salt

Instructions

Cream shortening, sugar and eggs. Add peanut butter and vanilla. Add flour, salt and baking soda. Form into small balls and press down with a fork dipped into milk. Bake at 350 degrees for 12 to 15 minutes.

item.title: Oatmeal Cookies
item.description:
• 1 cup margarine or butter
  • 1-1/2 cup brown sugar
  • 1 egg

Mix above ingredients well, then add:

Ingredients

  • 1-1/2 cup flour
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • 1-1/4 cup oatmeal

Instructions

Mix well and bake in 350 degree oven ‘til golden brown.

This recipe came from Florence Burton, who had gotten it from Roland Lejambe.

item.title: Oatmeal Snack Cookies
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2/3 cup butter or margarine, softened
  • 2/3 cup packed brown sugar
  • 2 eggs
  • 1-1/2 old-fashioned rolled oats
  • 1-1/2 cups all-purpose flour
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • ½ tsp salt
  • 1 6-oz pkg sweetened dried cranberries, option
  • 2/3 cup white or semi-sweet chocolate chips, optional

Instructions

Preheat oven to 375 F. Using electric mixer, beat better and sugar together in a medium mixing bowl until light and fluffy. Add eggs and mix well.

item.title: Never Fail Shortbread
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup Canadian Corn Starch
  • ½ cup icing sugar
  • 1 cup sifted all-purpose flour
  • ¾ cup butter (do not substitute)

Instructions

Sift cornstarch, icing sugar and flour together. Blend in butter with a spoon, mixing until a soft, smooth dough forms. Shape dough into 1” balls. Place about 1-1/2” apart on ungreased cookie sheet. Flatten with a lightly floured fork.

Bake in 300 F. (slow oven) for 20 – 25 minutes or until edges are slightly browned.

item.title: English Shortbread
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 lb butter
  • 1 cup fruit sugar
  • 4 cups flour

Instructions

Cream butter. Add sugar. Mix well. Add flour a little at a time, mixing well each time. Roll out ½ inch thick. Cut with cookie cutter. Bake on ungreased cookie sheet at 325 degree oven.

Bake 15 minutes. Turn cookie sheet. Continue baking for another 15 minutes.

Credit to Lorry Bernard.

item.title: Easy Macaroons
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 8-oz pkgs shredded coconut
  • 1 15-oz (1-1/3 cup) Eagle Brand
  • 2 tsp vanilla

Instructions

Preheat oven to 350 degrees. Mix ingredients. Drop from teaspoon onto well-greased cookie sheet. Bake at 350 degrees for 10 – 12 minutes. Cool slightly. Remove to rack. Makes about four dozen.

item.title: Easiest Chocolate Chip Cookies
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup light or dark brown sugar, packed
  • 1 large egg, slightly beaten
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • 1 cup chocolate chips

Instructions

Mix all ingredients together till well mixed. Drop by teaspoonful on a greased cookie sheet 2” apart.

Bake in a 350 degree oven till puffed and golden brown – about 10 minutes.

item.title: Dad's Cookies
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 cups raisins
  • 1 cup water
  • 1 cup lard or shortening
  • 2 cups sugar
  • 3 eggs, beaten
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • 3-1/2 cups all-purpose flour
  • 1 tsp baking powder
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • ½ tsp salt

Instructions

Cook raisins in water for five minutes. Cool. Cream lard or shortening and sugar. Add eggs, raisins and vanilla. Add sifted dry ingredients. Drop by spoonfuls about 1-1/2” apart on greased cookie sheet. Bake in 350 degree oven for 12 to 15 minutes.Oven grate on top – pan of water under.Remove cookies from pan immediately.

item.title: Cream Puffs
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup butter
  • 1 cup flour
  • 1 cup water
  • 4 eggs, unbeaten

Instructions

Heat butter and water. When butter is melted, add flour all at once and stir vigorously until mixture no longer sticks to sides of pan. Remove from stove, cool slightly; add eggs one at a time, beating after each egg is added. Drop by heaping tablespoons onto a well-greased baking sheet. Place 2 inches apart and bake in hot oven, 450 degrees F. for 15 minutes. Reduce heat to 325 degrees F., and bake about 20 minutes longer. Remove from baking sheet and cool.

item.title: Colette's Skeanut Butter Cookies
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup peanut butter (plain or chunky)
  • 1 cup sugar
  • 1 egg

Instructions

Mix egg and sugar together. Add peanut butter. Spoon onto greased cookie sheet. Press with a fork. Use small teaspoon and note that they really flatten and spread.

Bake at 350 F. for l0 minutes.

item.title: Coconut Macaroons
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3 pkg (200 gr ea) flaked coconut
  • 1 can (300 ml) Eagle Brand Milk
  • 2 tsp vanilla extract
  • 1-1/2 tsp almond extract

Instructions

  1. Combine coconut, Eagle Brand, vanilla and almond extracts; mix well.

  2. Drop by rounded spoonfuls onto well-greased cookie sheets.

  3. Bake 12 at a time on middle rack of preheated 325 F. oven, 10 – 12 minutes or until browned around the edges. Remove immediately to wire racks, cool completely.

item.title: Chocolate Coconut Macaroon Logs
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2/3 cup sweetened condensed milk
  • 1 Tbsp unsweetened cocoa powder
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • 3 cups flaked coconut
  • 2 egg whites
  • 8 oz. Semi-sweet chocolate, melted (optional)

Instructions

In large bowl, blend together condensed milk, cocoa and vanilla; stir in flaked coconut until well mixed. Beat egg whites until stiff peaks form; fold into coconut mixture.

Drop by tablespoonfuls onto lightly greased or parchment paper-lined baking sheet; shape into logs about 3” long. Bake at 300 degrees F for 30 minutes or until golden brown and firm. Let cool on wire racks. Dip one end of each log into chocolate (if using). Let stand on waxed paper until chocolate is set.

item.title: Chocolate Chip Cookies
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 2 eggs
  • ¼ cup white sugar
  • ¾ cup brown sugar
  • 1 cup margarine or butter
  • 2 cups chocolate chips
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • 1 pkg vanilla or chocolate instant pudding
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • 2-1/4 cups flour
  • Walnuts (optional)

Instructions

Mix egg, butter, sugars, vanilla.

Add instant pudding and the rest of the dry ingredients.

Bake for 10 minutes at 375 degree F. oven.

item.title: Butterscotch Shortbread Cookies
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ lb butter
  • ½ cup brown sugar
  • 2 cups flour

Instructions

Mix the ingredients together; roll out ½”thick.

Cut out with metal cookie cutters.

Bake 325 F. or 20 minutes on an ungreased cookie sheet.

NOTE FROM COLETTE PEPLINSKIE: I use a pastry cutter, then knead into a ball

item.title: Cherry Surprises
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup butter
  • ½ cup coconut
  • 1-1/2 cup icing sugar
  • 1 tsp milk
  • 1 tsp vanilla Pinch of salt
  • Cherries

Instructions

Cream butter, mix all ingredients and make into balls around cherries. Roll in graham wafers.

NOTE: If using maraschino cherries, drain them well and dry them or icing will stick to them.

item.title: Best Peanut Butter Cookies
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cup soft butter
  • 2 eggs
  • 1 cup peanut butter (Smooth or chunky)
  • 2-1/2 cups flour
  • 1 cup white sugar
  • 1 tsp baking powder
  • 1 cup brown sugar
  • ½ tsp salt

Instructions

Preheat oven to 350 degrees F. Use a wooden spoon or electric mixer to cream the butter in a large bowl. Add peanut butter and mix until well blended. Add white and brown sugars, a little at a time, mixing until they are blended in. Break an egg into a small bowl and stir. Add to the peanut butter mixture and mix well. Do the same with second egg. Sift flour, baking powder and salt over mixture; stir until well blended. Form into 1 to 1 inch balls. Place on greased cookie sheet. Flatten with a fork. Bake for 10 minutes. Makes 36 large cookies.

item.title: Beautiful Sugar Cookies
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup softened butter
  • ¾ cup sugar
  • 1 Medium or Large egg (not Extra large or Jumbo)
  • 1 Tbsp milk or cream
  • 2 cups flour
  • ¼ tsp salt
  • ¼ tsp nutmeg or cinnamon

Instructions

Thoroughly cream the butter by hand. Add sugar and mix until fluffy. Beat in the egg and then the milk.

In a separate bowl mix together all dry ingredients. Stir them into the butter. Knead the dough for just a minute. Chill the dough, then form the cookies as directed in the “General Instructions” and bake at 350 degrees for 10 – 12 minutes, or until the edges brown.

item.title: Beautiful Ginger Cookies
item.description:
Ingredients

  • ½ cup softened butter
  • ½ cup packed dark brown sugar
  • ¼ cup molasses
  • 1 medium or large egg (do not use extra large or jumbo)
  • 2-1/4 cups flour
  • ½ tsp cinnamon
  • 2 tsp ginger
  • 2 dashes cloves
  • 1 dash cayenne pepper

Instructions

Thoroughly cream the butter by hand. Add brown sugar and molasses, then the egg. In a separate bowl mix together all dry ingredients. Stir them into the butter mixture. Knead the dough briefly. Chill the dough, then form the cookies as directed in the “General Instructions” and bake at 375 degrees for 10 – 12 minutes, or until the edges brown.

item.title: Shortbread Cookies #2
item.description:
• 1 1/4 cups all purpose flour
  • 1/2 cup cornstarch
  • 1/2 cup icing sugar
  • 1 cup butter, softened
  • pinch of salt

Preheat oven to 275 degrees Fahrenheit.

In a medium bowl sift together cornstarch, icing sugar, and flour. Add butter; stir until smooth dough is formed.

Line a cookie sheet with parchment paper. Roll dough into 1-inch balls. Place two inches apart on cookie sheet. Flatten with your fingers or fork. Decorate with seasonal sprinkles.

item.title: Shortbread Cookies #1
item.description:
• 1 cup soft butter
  • 1 1/2 cups flour
  • 1/2 cup cornstarch
  • 1/2 cup icing sugar
  • 1 tsp vanilla or almond flavouring

Roll out or cookie press. Decorate with small pieces of red and green cherries. Bake in 325 degree Fahrenheit oven for around 15-20 mins.

NOTES:

Ingredients

  • I used margarine once and butter the next. MUCH better with real butter.
  • 4 out of 5

Credit to Camp Lau-Ren’s Camp Family Favourites Cookbook

item.title: Oatmeal Raisin Cookies
item.description:
• 3/4 brown sugar
  • 1/2 cup sugar
  • 3/4 cup butter
  • 2 eggs
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • 1 1/2 cups all purpose flour
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • 1/2 tsp salt
  • 1/2 tsp cinnamon
  • 2 cups quick cooking oats
  • 1 cup raisins

Preheat oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.

In a medium sized bowl blend together sugars and butter until light and fluffy.

Add eggs and vanilla; mix thoroughly.

In a separate bowl sift together flour, baking soda, salt, and cinnamon; stir into wet mixture. Add oatmeal and raisins; stir until mixed.

item.title: The Best Chocolate Chip Cookie Recipe Ever
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 cupsalted buttersoftened
  • 1 cupgranulated sugar
  • 1 cuplight brown sugarpacked
  • 2 teaspoonspure vanilla extract
  • 2 large eggs
  • 3 cupsall-purpose flour
  • 1 teaspoonbaking soda
  • ½ teaspoonbaking powder
  • 1 teaspoonsea salt
  • 2 cupschocolate chips(14 oz)

Instructions

  1. Preheat oven to 375 degrees F. Line three baking sheets with parchment paper and set aside.
  2. In a medium bowl mix flour, baking soda, baking powder and salt. Set aside.
  3. Cream together butter and sugars until combined.
  4. Beat in eggs and vanilla until light (about 1 minute).
  5. Mix in the dry ingredients until combined.
  6. Add chocolate chips and mix well.
  7. Roll 2-3 Tablespoons (depending on how large you like your cookies) of dough at a time into balls and place them evenly spaced on your prepared cookie sheets.
  8. Bake in preheated oven for approximately 8-10 minutes. Take them out when they are justbarelystarting to turn brown.
  9. Let them sit on the baking pan for 5 minutes before removing to cooling rack.

Billy’s Notes:  These are fantastic!  Not only are they pretty easy to make (not gummy dough, so easy to roll in balls…heh, heh, balls), but they are moist and taste kickass.  Keep an eye on them in the oven though.  I’d only done 7 mins and they were perfect.

item.title: Maya's Cookie Brownie Recipe
item.description:
Maya’s Cookie Brownie Recipe (Blondies)

Ingredients

  • ½ cup unsalted butter
  • 2 teaspoons vanilla
  • ¼ teaspoon to ½ salt
  • ½ cup packed brown sugar
  • ½ cup granulated sugar
  • 1 large egg
  • 1 cup all-purpose flour
  • ½ cup of chocolate chips

Instructions

Instructions:

  1. Preheat the oven to 350F degrees. Line an 8x8 inch square baking pan with aluminium foil leaving an overhang on each side and spray lightly with non-stick cooking spray. Or line the pan with parchment paper. Set aside.

item.title: Chewy Chocolate Cookies
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 1/4 cups butter, softened
  • 2 cups white sugar
  • 2 eggs
  • 2 teaspoons vanilla extract
  • 2 cups all-purpose flour
  • 3/4 cup unsweetened cocoa powder
  • 1 teaspoon baking soda
  • 1/2 teaspoon salt
  • 2 cups semisweet chocolate chips

Directions

  1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C).
  2. In a large bowl, cream together the butter and sugar until light and fluffy. Beat in the eggs one at a time, then stir in the vanilla. Sift together the flour, cocoa, baking soda, and salt; stir into the creamed mixture. Mix in the chocolate chips. Drop dough by teaspoonfuls onto ungreased cookie sheets.
  3. Bake 8 to 9 minutes in the preheated oven. Cookies will be soft. Cool slightly on cookie sheet; remove from sheet onto wire rack to cool completely.

Here is a link to the actual website. Credit to allrecipes.com:

item.title: Cinnamon Streusel Cookies
item.description:
Ingredients

Cookie Dough:

  • 1/2 cup softened butter
  • 1/2 cup vegetable oil
  • 1 cup brown sugar
  • 1 egg
  • 1 tsp vanilla
  • 2 tsp cinnamon
  • 1/2 tsp baking soda
  • 1/2 tsp baking powder
  • 1/2 tsp salt
  • 2 and 1/2 cups flour

Streusel Topping:

  • 1/4 cup softened butter
  • 1/4 cup brown sugar
  • 1/3-1/2 cup flour (depending on the consistency once mixed)
  • Pinch of salt

Icing:

  • 1/2 cup powdered sugar
  • 1 tbsp milk
  • splash of vanilla

Instructions

  1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
  2. Combine butter, oil, and brown sugar and mix for a few minutes until light and fluffy. Add egg and vanilla and mix again. Add remaining ingredients and mix until dough forms
  3. For streusel, combine ingredients and mix. If the mixture is too sticky and not crumbly, add more flour.
  4. Scoop 1/4 cup of dough and place on a tray with parchment paper. Make a small indent on the top of the dough ball and add streusel topping into the dent.
  5. Bake for 12-15 minutes. While baking make the icing, combine all ingredients and whisk until a runny icing/glaze forms.
  6. Add icing once cookies are cooled :)

item.title: Carrot Cake Cheesecake Cookies
item.description:
Ingredients

Cream Cheese Filling:

  • 1 block of cream cheese
  • 1/2 cup powdered sugar
  • 1 teaspoon vanilla extract

Cookie Dough:

  • 1 cup butter or margarine
  • 3/4 cup white sugar
  • 1/3 cup brown sugar
  • 1 egg
  • 2 teaspoons vanilla extract
  • 4 large carrots
  • 3 cups all purpose flour
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • 1/2 teaspoon baking powder
  • 1 tbsp cinnamon
  • 1 tsp nutmeg

Instructions

  1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit
  2. Mix room temp cream cheese, powdered sugar, and vanilla together in a bowl
  3. Scoop 1 tablespoon balls of the mixture onto parchment paper and chill in freezer while preparing cookie dough
  4. Beat butter, white sugar, and brown sugar together for about 1-2 minutes. Once combined, mix in egg and vanilla
  5. Shred, pat dry, and then mix carrots in.
  6. Fold in flour, baking powder, baking soda, cinnamon, and nutmeg.
  7. Scoop 1/3 cup of dough, flatten in palm of hand and place a cream cheese ball in the middle. Roll the dough around the cream cheese ball.
  8. Roll cookie dough ball in white sugar (optional)
  9. Place 6 to a tray with lots of room for expansion
  10. Cook 10 minutes, check them, 3 more minutes, check them, 3 more minutes
  11. Allow to cool ON TRAY for 10 minutes

item.title: Confetti Cheesecake Cookies
item.description:
Ingredients

Cream Cheese Filling:

  • 1 block of cream cheese
  • 1/2 cup powdered sugar
  • 1 teaspoon vanilla extract

Cookie Dough:

  • 1 cup butter or margarine
  • 1 and a 1/2 cups granulated sugar
  • 1 egg
  • 1 and a 1/2 teaspoon vanilla extract
  • 2 and 3/4 cups all purpose flour
  • 1 tsp baking soda
  • 1/2 teaspoon baking powder
  • 1/4 cup sprinkles

Instructions

  1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit
  2. Mix room temp cream cheese, powdered sugar, and vanilla together in a bowl
  3. Scoop 1 tablespoon balls of the mixture onto parchment paper and chill in freezer while preparing cookie dough
  4. Beat butter and sugar together for about 1-2 minutes. Once combined, mix in egg and vanilla
  5. Fold in flour, baking powder, and baking soda. Mix in sprinkles.
  6. Scoop 1/3 cup of dough, flatten in palm of hand and place a cream cheese ball in the middle. Roll the dough around the cream cheese ball.
  7. Roll cookie dough ball in white sugar (optional)
  8. Place 6 to a tray with lots of room for expansion
  9. Cook 10 minutes, check them, 3 more minutes, check them, 3 more minutes
  10. Allow to cool ON TRAY for 10 minutes

item.title: Coconut Cookies (Persian-Style) (Farang's)
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 1/2 cups of ground coconut
  • 1/3 cup of shredded coconut
  • 1 2/3 cups of powdered sugar
  • 2 large egg whites (approx. 1/4 cup)
  • 1 tsp salt
  • 1 tbsp lemon juice
  • 1/3 cup neutral oil (such as sunflower or canola)

Instructions

  1. In a bowl, combine the finely ground coconut and powdered sugar.

  2. Add the egg whites directly to the mixture. There is no need to whisk them beforehand. Mix until a soft, smooth dough forms.
